raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus on (A) language/style polish only, (B) content-level assessment of claims, novelty, and citations, or (C) a comprehensive review covering both?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A review should verify and, if needed, reframe the section’s thesis, gap, and contribution claims at a conceptual level.",
    "R1.2": "Assess the logical progression from background to stated contributions and results to ensure internal coherence.",
    "R1.3": "Check that performance and recall claims are adequately tied to the reported evaluations as described in the text.",
    "R1.4": "Evaluate how the proposed method differentiates from named prior graph-based and compression methods to gauge novelty.",
    "R1.6": "Provide sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements typical of section reviews.",
    "R1.7": "Verify that citations are accurate, appropriately attributed, and sufficiently cover key related work mentioned.",
    "RX.1": "Flag areas where judgments are uncertain due to missing details and qualify recommendations accordingly.",
    "RX.2": "Ensure the review discourages or corrects any overreaching statements relative to the evidence presented.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the review focused strictly on this section and the requested scope without expanding into full-paper tasks.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid guidance that could promote unethical scholarship or misleading presentation of results.",
    "RX.5": "Ground every comment in traceable references to specific sentences or claims in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on (A) language/style polish only, (B) content-level assessment of claims, novelty, and citations, or (C) a comprehensive review covering both?

rationale.R1.1: A review should verify and, if needed, reframe the section’s thesis, gap, and contribution claims at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: Assess the logical progression from background to stated contributions and results to ensure internal coherence.
rationale.R1.3: Check that performance and recall claims are adequately tied to the reported evaluations as described in the text.
rationale.R1.4: Evaluate how the proposed method differentiates from named prior graph-based and compression methods to gauge novelty.
rationale.R1.6: Provide sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements typical of section reviews.
rationale.R1.7: Verify that citations are accurate, appropriately attributed, and sufficiently cover key related work mentioned.
rationale.RX.1: Flag areas where judgments are uncertain due to missing details and qualify recommendations accordingly.
rationale.RX.2: Ensure the review discourages or corrects any overreaching statements relative to the evidence presented.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the review focused strictly on this section and the requested scope without expanding into full-paper tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid guidance that could promote unethical scholarship or misleading presentation of results.
rationale.RX.5: Ground every comment in traceable references to specific sentences or claims in the provided text.
